title: Apple and Notion Launch AI Agent Platforms | Tyler Folkman posted ...
full_text:
Two platforms just declared themselves the "agent OS" on the same day.
Two platforms just declared themselves the "agent OS" on the same day.
May 13, 2026. Apple and Notion both made moves that define where AI agents will live.
Apple is redesigning the App Store to host third-party AI agents. Not chatbots. Autonomous agents that spin up sub-apps, execute cross-app workflows, and act on your behalf across devices. The challenge: maintaining review policies when apps can rewrite themselves. AI apps already generated $900M in App Store fees last year. Projected $1B+ in 2026. That was chatbots. Agents are the next order of magnitude.
Notion launched a Developer Platform that doesn't build agents. It hosts yours. External Agents API. Database sync from any API into Notion. Custom code running on their infrastructure via Workers. A CLI built specifically for developers and coding agents. Claude, Codex, Decagon plug in natively.
Two strategies. Same conclusion. Agents need a platform.
Apple owns consumer distribution. Notion owns enterprise workflow.
The platform war for AI agents just started.
